issues.description: Large number of build artifacts and intermediate files are present under `build/` (e.g. many files under `build/app/intermediates/...`). These are generated files and bloat the repo.
issues.suggestion: Add appropriate entries to `.gitignore` (e.g. `build/`, `*/.cxx/`, `*/intermediates/`) and remove committed build artifacts from git history; keep only source files in the repo.

issues.description: Decompiled library/artifact sources and large tmp/jadx folders are checked in (example file has ~8666 lines). This massively increases repository size and contains third-party/generated code.
issues.suggestion: Remove `tmp/` and decompiled outputs from the repository and add them to `.gitignore`. Keep only source you maintain or add a clear script to regenerate these artifacts.

issues.description: Exact duplicate detected: this file's content is identical to `shared-code/lib/utils/snackbar_helper.dart` (shared implementation appears copied into two locations).
issues.suggestion: Consolidate to a single shared implementation (move to `shared-code` and import, or keep one canonical file). Remove the duplicate to avoid drift and duplication of fixes.

issues.description: Exact duplicate detected: `lib/models/program.dart` duplicates `shared-code/lib/models/program.dart`.
issues.suggestion: Keep one canonical model file (preferably in `shared-code`), update imports to use the single copy, and remove the duplicate to prevent inconsistent model drift.

issues.description: Exact duplicate detected: `lib/models/category.dart` duplicates `shared-code/lib/models/category.dart`.
issues.suggestion: Consolidate the model into one location and import that module from both places instead of maintaining duplicates.

issues.description: Exact duplicate detected: `lib/services/ssl_handler.dart` duplicates `shared-code/lib/services/ssl_handler.dart`.
issues.suggestion: Consolidate implementation into a single shared module and remove duplicate, or extract shared logic into a common package to avoid copy-paste.

issues.description: Very large file (~5347 lines). Long files make comprehension, testing, and maintenance difficult; they often hide overly long functions and mixed responsibilities.
issues.suggestion: Split into smaller widgets/components (presentation vs. logic), extract helpers and business logic into services/blocs/providers, and add tests for isolated pieces.

issues.description: Very large service file (~4124 lines). Likely contains multiple responsibilities and complex flows that are hard to read and test.
issues.suggestion: Break into smaller services, extract helpers and clear public APIs, and add unit tests. Consider dividing by responsibility (parsing, storage, scheduling) to improve maintainability.

issues.description: Large provider (~3771 lines). Complex providers can become brittle and hard to reason about.
issues.suggestion: Refactor into smaller providers or move heavy logic into services; implement clear interfaces and unit tests for extracted services.

issues.description: Large UI screen (~2125 lines). Big UI files mix layout, state management and business logic which complicates maintenance.
issues.suggestion: Decompose the screen into sub-widgets, keep state management in blocs/providers, and extract utility/view-model logic into separate files.

issues.description: Large service (~1762 lines). May combine network, parsing, caching and heuristics in a single file causing high complexity.
issues.suggestion: Split responsibilities (network fetch, caching, heuristics, scoring) into smaller, testable components and add unit tests for each piece.

issues.description: Relatively large service (several hundred lines). Potentially mixes orchestration and detailed processing logic.
issues.suggestion: Extract processing steps into smaller functions/classes and add unit tests for the complex logic paths; keep orchestration separate from processing.

issues.description: Repository contains a `TODO.md` and multiple TODO entries in docs mentioning known issues (e.g. 'Fix App Startup Issue'). These are outstanding actionable items.
issues.suggestion: Convert high-priority TODOs into tracked issues in the project tracker, add owners and ETA, and close/clean remaining TODO comments in code when addressed.

issues.description: File contains `// ignore_for_file: todo` (suppresses TODO lints). This can hide outstanding technical debt across the file.
issues.suggestion: Remove broad suppressions; address or triage TODOs in the file and use fine-grained suppressions only when necessary with a comment explaining why.

issues.description: Debug `print` call present: `print(message);` at line 26 (also at line 68). Leftover debug prints can clutter logs and leak data in production.
issues.suggestion: Replace `print` with structured logging (use a logging package) or remove, and ensure logging can be configured for levels in production.

issues.description: Debug `print` call present at line 68 (see also line 26).
issues.suggestion: Replace `print` with a configurable logger or remove for production.

issues.description: `print(flutterMessage);` present at line 223 — debug logging in application entrypoint.
issues.suggestion: Use a logging framework with configurable log levels; remove noisy prints from production code.

issues.description: `print(platformMessage);` present at line 240 — another debug print in startup path.
issues.suggestion: Replace with structured logging and guard sensitive information from logs.

issues.description: Decompiled/third-party code under `tmp/tivimate_jadx` contains many `catch (Exception|Throwable) { /* unused */ }` or broad empty catches. While these are in generated/decompiled code, they indicate swallowed exceptions patterns present in the repository.
issues.suggestion: Remove decompiled/third-party artifacts from the repository. If any third-party code is intentionally included, review and document exception handling practices; avoid broad empty catches in maintained code.

issues.description: Multiple directories (`lib/` and `shared-code/`) contain overlapping implementations (e.g. snackbar helper, models). This inconsistent layout increases risk of drift and confusion about canonical sources.
issues.suggestion: Establish a single canonical source location (e.g. `shared-code`) and update imports; consider creating a small internal package for shared code to avoid duplicates and improve clarity.
